mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into higher equity valuations and lower short-rate discounting, so sustained declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive more often than not during months when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already elevated, a further rate increase tightens discounting and credit conditions, which should depress S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases after FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which should reduce expected corporate revenues and lower S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negative on average after unemployment rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, or if hit rate exceeds 60% positive in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS signals broadening nominal demand and payroll income, which should support sales growth and lift S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term does not outperform single-series effects in walk-forward tests, or if 20-day forward returns are not positive when both RSAFS and PAYEMS are above their 3-month trends.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and capacity utilization, which strengthens earnings expectations and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in walk-forward samples, or if the signal reverses sign during expansion periods without a corresponding recession marker.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST is below trend and RSAFS is also weakening, the economy is likely losing demand momentum across housing and retail channels, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST below 6-month trend and RSAFS below 3-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not show negative average 20-day returns, or if either HOUST or RSAFS alone explains the full effect without the joint condition improving fit.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation regime, a downward monthly CPIAUCSL trend reduces expected policy pressure and real-rate uncertainty, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-inflation episodes defined by CPIAUCSL trend below 2% do not correspond to above-baseline 60-day forward returns, or if the sign turns negative for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: A labor-market mix with rising PAYEMS and stable or falling UNRATE implies payroll growth without deterioration in slack, which should support earnings and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 0 and UNRATE flat or down
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods fail to beat unconditional 20-day return rates, or if positive PAYEMS growth with flat/falling UNRATE is followed by negative returns in a majority of occurrences.
